title: SpecGate Binding File
description: Schema for SpecGate binding files (v0.4.0). Connects a spec to a language-specific implementation. All paths are relative to the binding file's location.

language.description: Target language identifier. Selects the codegen backend.

targets.description: Named execution targets. Each key is a target name. Specs select targets via the 'target' field. When omitted, the harness uses the first target.

targetDefinition.description: An execution target pointing to a package/crate with execution instructions.

package_root.description: Path to the package/crate/project root, relative to the binding file. The harness runs builds and resolves source from here.

test_root.description: Path where generated test files are written, relative to the binding file. Defaults to package_root/tests if omitted.

module.description: Module path within the package that contains the annotated operations. Used in generated code as 'use <module> as fut;'. Defaults to the crate root.

function.description: Fully qualified function to call for API-style targets. E.g. 'specgate_harness::run_spec'. Mutually exclusive with 'command'.

command.description: Shell command to execute for command-style targets. Exit 0 = pass. Supports {spec_path} and {workdir} templates. Mutually exclusive with 'function'.

constructor.description: Fully qualified constructor for the receiver type (for targets where operations are methods on an object).

env.description: Environment variables to set when running this target. Keys are variable names, values are strings.

build.description: Optional build command to run before execution. Runs from package_root.

outputs.description: Where to read structured output from after execution.

file.description: Path to output file. Supports {workdir} template.

oneOf.description: API target — invokes a function directly.
Command target — runs a shell command.
Package-only target — used for build/check validation. Neither function nor command.
